concerns.description: Complete section or page missing
concerns.analysis: There is a catastrophic pipeline failure resulting in the complete loss of 23 pages from the output. Entire core sections of the document, including '1.0 Introduction', '2.0 Objectives of RMP', '3.0 Summary of Approved Risk Assessment Report', '4.0 RMP Components', and multiple critical legal notices in the appendices are entirely missing.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
1.0 Introduction
Hart & Hickman, PC (H&H) has prepared this Risk Management Plan (RMP) for the Galaxy Cleaners site on behalf of the North Carolina Dry-Cleaning Solvent Cleanup Act (DSCA) Program.

concerns.description: Images missing or broken
concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains several highly meaningful images that are missing from the HTML output. First, the 'PCE Concentration vs. Time' line charts on pages 18 and 19 are completely missing, represented only by placeholder text describing them (e.g., '[Image: A line chart...]') without any actual element. Second, the detailed 'REDUCTION OF SURVEY PLAT' maps on pages 34 and 43 are entirely omitted from the HTML; the output simply jumps from the 'REDUCTION OF SURVEY PLAT' heading straight to
concerns.source_pdf_quote: 1) PCE Concentration vs. Time line charts (pages 18-19). 2) Survey Plat maps (pages 34 and 43).
concerns.output_quote:
1)

[Image: A line chart displaying PCE Concentration vs. Time...]

(placeholder text instead of actual image). 2)

REDUCTION OF SURVEY PLAT

immediately followed by

Appendix D

, omitting the plat map entirely.
concerns.location: Pages 18-19 and Pages 34 & 43 / HTML sections following 'Appendix A' and 'Appendix C'

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly
concerns.analysis: The source document contains a Table of Contents with page number references. The pipeline extracted this Table of Contents as a table and explicitly retained the 'Page No.' column and the corresponding page numbers. Because HTML is a continuous document without page boundaries, these retained page number references provide dead navigation to users, violating structural conversion guidelines.

concerns.description: Content visually duplicated on page
concerns.analysis: The HTML output contains a full-page screenshot of the source PDF's cover page (Page 1) embedded as an image alongside the extracted text for that same page. The user sees the title and document metadata twice: once as structured HTML text and a second time inside the large element rendered immediately beneath it.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Cover page text: 'Risk Management Plan Galaxy Cleaners... January 15, 2016' (Page 1)
concerns.output_quote:
Extracted text '

Risk Management Plan

...

H&H Project No. DS0-85A
January 15, 2016

' followed immediately by '

concerns.description: Significant word count loss: 42% of baseline
concerns.analysis: baseline=10522, output=4399

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 1 table(s) with matching structure across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=1. Examples: pages 16-17: 21 cols (table 8: analytical data for groundwater, adt 8...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements. In HTML, these should be one continuous table to maintain accessibility and reading order.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 2 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis:
caption_as_header=2. Examples: p14: "TITLE
GROUNDWATER CONTAMINANT CONCENTRATION MAP"; p15: "TITLE
SOIL CONTAMINANT
CONCENTRATION MAP". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Missing images: 2 of 10 source image(s) not present in output
concerns.analysis: image_count_source=10, html_img_count=8, image_count_ratio=2.6. Some source images are missing from the output.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 6 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=6. Examples: p1: "Document cover page for Galaxy Cleaners located at"; p1: "Title page of a report for Galaxy Cleaners at Wave"; p1: "Document cover page for Galaxy Cleaners at Waverly". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (14): 2923 South Tryon Street, Suite 100
Charlotte, NC 28203
704.5; 3334 Hillsborough Street
Raleigh, NC 27607
919.847.4241 main; i
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=14, pages_affected=11/54. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 1 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: www.ncdsca.org,
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=1. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 1 ordered list(s) have alphabetical markers (A, B, C) rendered as numeric — e.g. "a. child care centers or schools; or"
concerns.analysis: alpha_numeric_confusion=1. Ordered list items start with sequential letter prefixes (A., B., C.) but are rendered as numeric lists, losing the alphabetical semantics.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 2 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=0, no_th_tables=2. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 5 form(s) with 11 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=5, total_fields=11. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 20 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (20 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...ted at Waverly Place Shopping Center in Cary, NC ("; p1: "...aners at Waverly Place Shopping Center in Cary, NC"; p1: "...erly Place Shopping Center in Cary, NC, labeled DS"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=20 (truncated=20, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

concerns.description: Table column count mismatch: p17(source:21cols,output:22cols)
concerns.analysis: Matched tables have different column counts. Pages: p17(source:21cols,output:22cols)

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "cc: dsca site # dc920029 file"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages in first/last position

concerns.description: Degenerate images detected: 8 images with extreme aspect ratio or tiny dimensions
concerns.analysis: degenerate_count=8, threshold=2

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 1 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=1, examples=['https://www.harthickman.com']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.
